checked_queries: DKMS RPC validation enforces executor and TTL only
negative_search_evidence.missing_behavior: validate_dkms_key_payload does more than executor/TTL-only validation: it validates base encrypted secret/public key fields and rejects zero owner.
related_but_insufficient_code: crates/ritual-precompile-codecs/src/validation.rs validates executor, TTL, encrypted_secrets, user_public_key, and owner nonzero for DKMS.
crates/ritual-precompile-codecs/src/dkms_key.rs decodes owner/key fields but validation intentionally rejects owner zero.

notes: The current port implements the later owner-required DKMS behavior, so this older validation-only invariant is not preserved.
rationale: The validator explicitly states DKMS uses base executor/TTL validation and leaves potential DKMS-specific owner or key-format checks as future additions. No candidate in this bounded assignment contradicts or removes it.
statement: validate_dkms_key_payload must decode DkmsKeyRequest, reject zero executor, ttl 0, and ttl greater than max_ttl_blocks(), and otherwise accept the request without owner, key_index, or key_format range checks beyond ABI decoding.
suggested_falsification_tests: Call validate_dkms_key_payload with a valid DKMS request and assert Ok; then test zero executor, ttl zero, ttl max_ttl_blocks()+1, and malformed ABI and assert ExecutorZeroAddress, TTLZero, TTLExceeded, and DecodeFailed respectively. Also assert owner zero and arbitrary key_index pass when ABI decoding succeeds.
summary: DKMS validation is stricter than executor-and-TTL-only.
test_or_falsification_candidate: Call validate_dkms_key_payload with a valid DKMS request and assert Ok; then test zero executor, ttl zero, ttl max_ttl_blocks()+1, and malformed ABI and assert ExecutorZeroAddress, TTLZero, TTLExceeded, and DecodeFailed respectively. Also assert owner zero and arbitrary key_index pass when ABI decoding succeeds.
title: DKMS RPC validation enforces executor and TTL only
